videos.title: CSP NAKKERTOK 2017
videos.description:
Nakkertok's volunteer ski patrol team is part of the Canadian Ski Patrol (CSP).  

Members of the Canadian Ski Patrol assigned to Nakkertok receive their training and insurance through the CSP, and adhere to national standards for Advanced First Aid.  

The next training session starts Saturday October 14 and runs on Saturdays (8-3 pm) until December 2.  

The condensed course starts November 2 and runs until December 2, on Saturdays (8-4 pm) plus Tuesday and Thursday evenings.  

Students are taught together in the City of Gatineau at the Pierre Elliot Trudeau Elementary School, and must cover a $200 insurance fee and some of their equipment costs.  

The local volunteer recruitment contact is Tatiana, whose phone number is 819-923-5218, her email is tatiana.zabelina@icloud.com    

Patrol schedules at Nakkertok are set in collaboration with the entire Nakkertok patrol team to try to find schedules that meet everyone's needs.  We cover Nakkertok's race events wearing boots, and the Learn to Ski program wearing skis.

Additional questions can be sent to John, Assistant Patrol Leader, Nakkertok, at john@mymason.ca

videos.title: The American Birkebeiner cross-country ski race
videos.description:
Since it was first held 50 years ago, the American Birkebeiner, a trek of more than 30 miles through the Northwoods of Wisconsin, modeled after a fabled bit of Norwegian history, has become the largest cross-country ski race in the U.S. Correspondent Conor Knighton reports on how enthusiasm for the festival of events surrounding the race (dubbed "Birke fever") has snowballed.
#Birkebeiner #crosscountryski 

"CBS Sunday Morning" features stories on the arts, music, nature, entertainment, sports, history, science and Americana, and highlights unique human accomplishments and achievements. Check local listings for CBS Sunday Morning broadcast times.

videos.title: Loppet in Red Deer
videos.description:
Red Deer School loppet About 450 children from Red Deer and area schools descended on River Bend Recreation area on Tuesday for the 27th Annual Red Deer School Loppet. Competing in three and five kilometre events, the skiers made their way around the trails on mass before ending at the parking lot where some were awarded medals for their effort. For those who didn't finish in the top three of the various grade categories the fun of the event and the cookie and juice at the finish line were enough to make almost everyone happy.

For full story see:
http://www.albertalocalnews.com/reddeeradvocate/news/local/Loppet_84590627.html
